entity.name: Mohd Kaif
entity.description:
Solo AI engineer and the full-time maintainer of Semantica. I left my job to focus entirely on this project after recognising a clear and unmet need in the open-source ecosystem: developers building AI systems had no solid, production-ready framework for semantic reasoning, knowledge graphs, and explainable decision tracking.

Over the past year I have shipped 29 production-ready modules, 50+ Jupyter notebook tutorials, integrations with FAISS, Neo4j, AWS Neptune, Apache AGE, and Docling, and a complete W3C PROV-O compliant provenance layer — all without any external funding. Everything has been self-funded and self-maintained.

This grant would allow me to continue full-time for the next 12 months: completing the roadmap, stabilising APIs, growing the community, and keeping Semantica free and open for everyone.

projects.name: Semantica
projects.description:
Semantica is a free, MIT-licensed Python framework that gives AI applications a proper semantic layer — the missing piece between raw text and trustworthy AI.

The problem: Modern AI systems (RAG pipelines, agents, multi-agent workflows) treat knowledge as bags of text chunks. They have no understanding of entities, relationships, rules, or decision history. In high-stakes domains — healthcare, finance, legal, cybersecurity — this leads to black-box answers that cannot be explained, audited, or trusted.

What Semantica does: It provides a complete, end-to-end pipeline from raw data to governed, explainable AI output:
• Universal ingestion — 50+ file formats (PDF, DOCX, HTML, CSV, databases, APIs)
• Semantic extraction — named entity recognition, relationship extraction, event detection
• Knowledge graph construction — with conflict detection, deduplication, and entity resolution
• Automatic ontology generation — 6-stage LLM pipeline producing OWL ontologies
• GraphRAG — hybrid vector + graph retrieval for 30%+ accuracy gains over plain RAG
• Decision tracking — full lifecycle management with precedent search, causal analysis, and policy compliance
• Provenance tracking — W3C PROV-O compliant lineage from source document to AI response
• Change management — versioned knowledge graphs with SHA-256 integrity verification

What makes it different: Semantica is not another wrapper around LangChain. It is the semantic intelligence layer that sits beneath your agents and makes them auditable, explainable, and trustworthy. It integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, CrewAI, and other frameworks rather than replacing them.

Traction: Available on PyPI, actively downloaded, MIT-licensed, 29 production-ready modules, 50+ tutorial notebooks spanning 14 real-world use-case domains (biomedical, finance, cybersecurity, supply chain, government, autonomous systems).

This funding will sustain full-time development for 12 months, enabling API stabilisation, expanded integrations, performance improvements, and community growth.

Support Semantica through GitHub Sponsors with recurring or one-time payments.

plans.name: FLOSS/fund Grant — 12 Months Full-Time Development
plans.description:
One-time grant to sustain 12 months of full-time, open-source development of Semantica.

Budget breakdown:
• Maintainer living costs (12 months) — $38,000: Allows full-time focus with no pressure to take on consulting or employment, ensuring the project moves forward consistently.
• Infrastructure & tooling — $5,000: CI/CD runners, GPU compute for embedding and ontology generation tests, cloud graph database instances (Neptune, Neo4j Aura) used for integration testing, PyPI and documentation hosting.
• Documentation, tutorials & community — $4,500: Writing domain-specific cookbooks, video walkthroughs, developer onboarding guides, and attending or presenting at one open-source conference.
• Dependency licences & third-party tools — $2,500: Paid APIs (OpenAI, Groq) used for automated integration tests and cookbook generation.

Total: $50,000 USD

Deliverables over 12 months:
1. Stable v1.0 release with frozen public API and migration guides
2. Full test coverage (unit + integration) across all 29 modules
3. Performance benchmarks published for GraphRAG vs. plain RAG across 5 domains
4. Expanded integrations: Weaviate, Qdrant, ChromaDB, Pinecone vector stores; Anthropic and Gemini LLM providers
5. Healthcare and finance compliance guides (HIPAA, SOX) with example implementations
6. Active community: Discord, GitHub Discussions, monthly release notes

Why $50,000 and not more: This is a lean, realistic budget for one dedicated maintainer. It covers living costs and project costs without waste. The ask is deliberately modest to demonstrate accountability and to fit within what is sustainable for a solo open-source project at this stage.

plans.name: Core Development Support (Monthly Sponsors)
plans.description: Monthly sponsorship to support ongoing full-time development of Semantica. Covers maintainer time, infrastructure, and tooling on a sustainable recurring basis.

plans.name: Infrastructure Support (Monthly Sponsors)
plans.description: Covers hosting, CI/CD, cloud database instances, GPU compute for tests, and automation pipelines used in development and integration testing.

history.description: Fully self-funded. No external grants, investors, or sponsorships. All development carried out using personal savings after leaving employment to work on Semantica full-time.
